evals.prompt: Write the viewport meta tag for a mobile page without blocking zoom.
evals.expected_output: Uses a normal viewport meta tag and avoids user-scalable=no or maximum-scale=1.
expectations: Zoom is not blocked
The viewport tag does not disable scaling
The solution is safe for low-vision users
The meta tag uses standard responsive settings

evals.prompt: Build a map control that supports pinch zoom but also works with a single pointer.
evals.expected_output: Provides visible Zoom in and Zoom out buttons as the single-pointer alternative to pinch zoom.
expectations: The functionality has a single-pointer alternative
The alternative is visible and operable
The control does not rely only on pinch gestures
The button labels are explicit

evals.prompt: Write a reorderable list where items can be moved without dragging.
evals.expected_output: Includes Move up and Move down buttons so the same result is possible without drag and drop.
expectations: A drag alternative is provided
The alternative uses buttons or clicks
The reordering result is possible without dragging
The list remains usable with keyboard and touch

evals.prompt: Write CSS for a small icon button that still meets touch target guidance.
evals.expected_output: Uses min-width and min-height or enough padding to provide a 44x44 CSS pixel hit area.
expectations: The clickable area is large enough for touch use
The target uses padding or minimum dimensions
The visible icon can remain small while the hit area grows
Primary actions should meet the 44x44 recommendation

evals.prompt: Write a button handler for deleting an item that must not fire on pointer down.
evals.expected_output: Uses click or another up-event equivalent so the user can cancel before the action completes.
expectations: The action completes on pointer up rather than pointer down
The user can cancel the action by moving away
mousedown and touchstart are not used for the final action
The code respects pointer cancellation guidance
